abstract:
nature synthesis

Article https://doi.org/10.1038/s44160-023-00351-1

Miniaturization of popular reactions
from the medicinal chemists’ toolbox for
ultrahigh-throughput experimentation

Received: 17 August 2022 Nathan Gesmundo 1,4, Kevin Dykstra2, James L. Douthwaite 3,
 Yu-Ting Kao 3, Ruheng Zhao 3, Babak Mahjour 3, Ron Ferguson2,
Accepted: 22 May 2023
 Spencer Dreher2, Bérengère Sauvagnat1,5, Josep Saurí1,6 & Tim Cernak 1,3,7

Published online: 22 June 2023

 Check for updates Miniaturization is a tactic employed in many technologies to accelerate
 discovery through parallelized data collection. The miniaturization of
 chemical synthesis to the limits of chemoanalytical and bioanalytical
 detection could accelerate drug discovery by increasing the amount of
 experimental data collected per milligram of material consumed. Here we
 demonstrate the miniaturization of popular reactions used in drug discovery
 such as reductive amination, N-alkylation, N-Boc (tert-butyloxycarbonyl)
 deprotection and Suzuki coupling for utilization in 1.2 µl reaction droplets.

methods_summary: Our development of a Suzuki coupling protocol can be handled without substantial evaporation during experiment for ultraHTE was inspired by prior reports detailing the nanoscale setup. evidence_chunks.text: Miniaturization of popular reactions from the medicinal chemists toolbox for ultrahigh-throughput experimentation

Received: 17 August 2022 Nathan Gesmundo 1,4, Kevin Dykstra2, James L. Douthwaite 3, Yu-Ting Kao 3, Ruheng Zhao 3, Babak Mahjour 3, Ron Ferguson2, Accepted: 22 May 2023 Spencer Dreher2, B reng re Sauvagnat1,5, Josep Saur 1,6 & Tim Cernak 1,3,7

Check for updates Miniaturization is a tactic employed in many technologies to accelerate discovery through parallelized data collection. The miniaturization of chemical synthesis to the limits of chemoanalytical and bioanalytical detection could accelerate drug discovery by increasing the amount of experimental data collected per milligram of material consumed. Here we demonstrate the miniaturization of popular reactions used in drug discovery such as reductive amination, N-alkylation, N-Boc (tert-butyloxycarbonyl) deprotection and Suzuki coupling for utilization in 1.2 l reaction droplets.
